title: Villa East Egg — Architectural Masterpiece by Kulapat Yantrasast
blurb: A once-in-a-generation acquisition on Phuket's tranquil east coast, with sea views over the estate's gardens and pools: a private residence conceived as a work of art by internationally acclaimed architect Kulapat Yantrasast — protégé of Tadao Ando, founder of WHY Architecture, and the designer behind the Metropolitan Museum's Rockefeller Wing and the Louvre's most significant recent transformation. Set on a 3,480 m² gated estate with 1,800 m² of internal space across three structures — five bedrooms, eight bathrooms, two pools and significant art-display architecture throughout. Original asking US$8.31M; now offered by auction from US$2M — message us today to register to bid.
detail:
Villa East Egg is not simply a home; it is a landmark work of contemporary architecture. Privately commissioned as a work of art rather than a house, it was conceived by Kulapat Yantrasast — protégé of Tadao Ando and founder of WHY Architecture — an architect globally recognised for the Rockefeller Wing at the Metropolitan Museum of Art in New York and for leading the Louvre's most significant museographic transformation in over a decade. The result is an immersive architectural statement designed around art, light and landscape, of exceptional scale, vision and provenance.

The estate occupies 3,480 m² of land with roughly 1,800 m² of internal living space, arranged across three independent structures: the principal residence, a self-contained guest pavilion, and a garage building with staff accommodation and plant facilities. In total the property offers five bedrooms and eight bathrooms, set within a secure, discreet gated estate of manicured lawns and architecturally designed landscaping, with sea views across the grounds.

The main villa unfolds over three levels connected by a central elevator, with dramatic volumes and carefully curated entertaining spaces. The upper level holds the grand living and dining spaces and a secondary kitchen, opening to elevated views and seamless indoor-outdoor flow; the first floor is given entirely to an exceptional principal suite with a luxury bathroom and separate bathing and shower areas; the ground floor adds two en-suite bedrooms, a private cinema room and a designer Vipp kitchen. An entry courtyard, swimming pool, wraparound terraces and landscaped gardens complete the composition.

The independent guest studio is a fully self-contained residence — two en-suite bedrooms, three bathrooms, its own living accommodation, a private swimming pool, wraparound terraces and an outdoor pool shower — offering complete privacy and flexibility. A separate garage building provides secure vehicle accommodation, staff quarters with a private bathroom, laundry and the estate's operational infrastructure.

The setting balances seclusion with access: roughly 25 km (about 20 minutes) from Phuket International Airport and 20 km (about 15 minutes) from two leading international schools, with all three of the island's marinas and four of its seven championship golf courses within a 25 km reach.

The property is sold unfurnished; any furniture, fixtures, decorative items and artwork are excluded from the sale and available only by separate negotiation. Offered by auction — original asking price US$8,310,000, with bidding from US$2,000,000. Post-auction, a Reservation Agreement is signed (by 23 July 2026) affording a 30-day due-diligence period, with the Share Sale & Purchase Agreement executed on completion.

Registration is required to take part. To register to bid, message us today — our team will walk you through the reservation and due-diligence process and confirm your place ahead of the auction.
internalName: Villa East Egg (WHY Architecture, The Phuket Residence) — auction from $2M
